# weekly_topic_dump

Individual call records from the CanadaLogin support queue, with topic
classifications for each call.

Each row represents one topic record associated with a single call. A single
call may have multiple rows if multiple topics were recorded.

`Keywords`: call centre, topics, call reasons, contact drivers, caller issues

## Provenance

Sourced from a weekly Excel report that is typically send to the EDCP Data and Research 
team via email. That spreadsheet is exctracted and loaded into the data repository via
the [call_centre_etl.py](https://github.com/cds-snc/canadalogin-ibm-verify-pipeline/blob/main/infra/modules/upload_source/call_centre_etl.py)
Lambda. The `extracted_at` field records when each row was last extracted 
from the source file.

* **Updated**: Weekly, typically on Tuesdays
* **Steward**: EDCP Data and Research Team
* **Contact**: [Colin Douglas](mailto:colin.douglas@cds-snc.ca)

## Fields

One row per topic record per call. A single call (`call_no`) may appear on
multiple rows if more than one topic was recorded.

| Field Name | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| call_no | integer | Identifier for the call. Not unique - multiple rows share the same `call_no` when multiple topics were recorded |
| call_datetime | integer | Call timestamp stored as a Unix nanosecond integer. Cast to timestamp before use |
| call_date | date | Date the call took place |
| date | string | Call date as a text date. Mirrors `call_date` |
| clienttype | string | Type of caller, for example "Business", "Public", "Government of Canada" |
| language | string | Language of the call, for example "English", "French" |
| origin | string | Province or region the caller is calling from |
| statobject | string | The call topic as recorded in the source system. May contain leading/trailing whitespace or inconsistent spacing |
| cstat | string | Full topic classification path, including category and topic (for example, "Main Document description::What is CanadaLogin?") |
| transactiontype | string | Type of transaction recorded for the topic, for example "Search", "Callback" |
| programcontact | string | Program contact information provided to the caller, if any. Often missing |
| source | float | Unused |

## Notes

Because a single call can generate multiple topic rows, do not count rows to get
call volume. Use `COUNT(DISTINCT call_no)` or join to
[`weekly_activity_report`](#weekly_activity_report) for call counts.

`statobject` frequently contains leading/trailing whitespace and repeated
internal spaces. Always clean it before grouping:

```sql
REGEXP_REPLACE(TRIM(statobject), '\s+', ' ') AS call_topic
```

See the [Call Centre metrics](../../02-call-centre.qmd) chapter for topic count
and topic share example SQL.
